title: Web Login Widget (OAuth2-Compatible Third-Party Authentication via Platform Identity)
description: Embeddable JavaScript widget allowing third-party websites to use the platform as an identity provider. User clicks 'Login with [Platform]', authorizes on a platform-hosted consent screen, and the website receives a signed JWT with the user's public profile. Follows OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code flow with PKCE. Never exposes internal session tokens to third-party origin.

security_model.flow: Authorization Code + PKCE (RFC 7636). Implicit flow EXPLICITLY PROHIBITED.

pkce.binding: Server stores code_challenge alongside auth_code. On token exchange, verifier hash must match — eliminates auth code interception attacks.

security_model.state_parameter: CSRF protection: client generates 32-byte random state, server validates on callback.
security_model.nonce: Optional: client sets nonce in auth request; server includes in id_token — prevents id_token replay.

token_types.access_token: Short-lived JWT (1h). Signed with ES256 (ECDSA P-256). Contains: sub (user_id hash), name, username, photo_url, iat, exp, aud (client_id).
token_types.refresh_token: Opaque 256-bit token. Stored hashed (SHA-256) in DB. TTL 30 days. Rotation on each use.
token_types.id_token: OIDC-compatible JWT with user profile claims.

token_security: Access token signed with per-environment ES256 key (rotated quarterly, JWKS endpoint published).
Refresh token rotation: each use invalidates previous token (chained rotation). Stolen token detection via reuse of invalidated token.
Refresh token family invalidation: if reuse detected → entire refresh token family revoked for that user+client.

schema.name: VARCHAR(128)
schema.is_verified: BOOLEAN (for 'Verified App' badge on consent screen)

client_registration.redirect_uri_policy: Exact match only. Wildcard redirect URIs are a critical OAuth vulnerability — categorically rejected at registration.

authorize.params: client_id, redirect_uri, scope, response_type=code, state, code_challenge, code_challenge_method=S256
authorize.consent_screen: Platform-hosted. Shows: requesting app name, logo, permissions. User approves or denies.

server_validation: code not previously used (mark as used atomically)

authorization_endpoints.jwks: GET /.well-known/jwks.json — public key set for token verification. Cached by clients, rotated via kid field.

scopes.phone: verified phone number (requires explicit user consent dialog)
scopes.email: verified email (requires explicit user consent dialog)

widget_security.csp: Widget JS loaded from platform CDN with SRI hash. Third-party sites must include script with integrity attribute.
widget_security.frame_origin: Consent popup opened in platform domain window — never iframed (clickjacking prevention).
widget_security.postmessage: Widget communicates result via window.postMessage with origin validation.

rate_limiting.authorize_endpoint: 20 req/min per IP, 5 req/min per client_id

alerts: oauth.refresh_token.reuse_detected > 5/min → PagerDuty P1 (possible token theft campaign)
